title: User navigates to the 'Your good deed for today' screen
test_steps: Open the app and navigate to the 'Your good deed for today' screen.
expected_result: The screen displays the title 'Your good deed for today' and the subtitle 'Today, your good deed is...'

title: User reads the body text explaining the concept of spiritual adoption
test_steps: Open the app and navigate to the 'Your good deed for today' screen. | Read the body text explaining the concept of spiritual adoption, voluntary sacrifices, and supporting women who have withdrawn from abortion.
expected_result: The body text is clearly visible and legible.

title: User reads the information on donating
test_steps: Open the app and navigate to the 'Your good deed for today' screen. | Read the information on donating as little as 10PLN to cover the cost of 20 diapers for children saved from abortion.
expected_result: The donation information is clearly visible and legible.

title: User navigates to the footer and reads the instructions to donate
test_steps: Open the app and navigate to the 'Your good deed for today' screen. | Scroll down to the footer and read the instructions to donate to the Tiny Feet Foundation.
expected_result: The footer instructions are clearly visible and legible.

title: User checks the accessibility of the screen
test_steps: Open the app and navigate to the 'Your good deed for today' screen. | Perform a screen reader test to ensure all text and elements are accessible.
expected_result: All text and elements are accessible and readable by a screen reader.

title: User checks the usability of the screen
test_steps: Open the app and navigate to the 'Your good deed for today' screen. | Perform a usability test to ensure the screen is easy to navigate and understand.
expected_result: The screen is easy to navigate and understand.

title: User checks the data persistence of the screen
test_steps: Open the app and navigate to the 'Your good deed for today' screen. | Close the app and reopen it to check if the screen data is still present.
expected_result: The screen data is still present when the app is reopened.
